description: Generic SQL-touching projects (detected loosely, via any *.sql file present). Also serves as the DB/ direction's vertical base - if a database-specific template (e.g. postgres-specific, oracle-specific) is ever added as a sibling under DB/, it will inherit this file automatically via directory nesting, no `extends` needed.
_notes: CORRECTION: an earlier version of this file declared 'oracledb@claude-plugins-official' as a plugin. Re-verified directly against the anthropics/claude-plugins-official repo's plugin+external_plugins directory listing (GitHub API, not a search-engine summary): this id DOES NOT EXIST. No oracledb, postgres, mysql, redis, mongodb, cloud-sql, alloydb, or neon plugin exists anywhere in that catalog - the claim was wrong, not just an Oracle-vs-Postgres scoping choice. Removed.

No generic database plugin exists in the official Claude Code marketplace at all, for any RDBMS. Use MCP SERVERS instead (a different config surface from enabledPlugins/`claude plugin install` - added via `claude mcp add` or your client's connector settings), all free/self-hosted-friendly and independently verified as of this writing:
Postgres: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers (postgres server, read-only by default, official reference implementation).
RabbitMQ: github.com/kenliao94/mcp-server-rabbitmq (community project - check recent commit activity before adopting).
Kafka: no single dominant/official option - github.com/tuannvm/kafka-mcp-server or github.com/Joel-hanson/kafka-mcp-server, pick by activity at adoption time.

Avoid cloud-managed-only variants (Neon, AlloyDB, Cloud SQL) if the goal is a genuinely free setup - prefer self-hosted Postgres/Redis via the MCP servers above; a free client-side tool talking to an inherently paid managed service isn't 'free' in practice.
